source.filename: Tinea Pedis : Athletes Foot (acute - vesiculobullous) DST-045.txt

attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 DST (Dermatology & Soft Tissue) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Bidirectional tier calibration reviewed and accepted: benign / self-limiting dermatoses held low-tier (T1/T2, self-care + routine referral), while skin malignancy (melanoma/SCC/BCC), spreading soft-tissue infection, limb-threat (critical limb ischaemia, diabetic foot ulcer) and airway/anaphylaxis risk were escalated to true acuity (T3/T4) with mandatory escalation edges; no autonomous diagnosis, no paediatric dosing.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all DST casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: case_id SEQ is provisional (SPEC-DERM-01-00245 derived from source DST-045) — maintainer to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-DERM-01 bucket at ingest (--reseq).

Age not stated in source; demographics.age is schema-required so a representative value of 42 was entered as a placeholder and age_band set to adult, inferred from occupational occlusive footwear (heavy boots, thick wool socks), hyperhidrosis and 78 kg body weight — please confirm/correct the exact age on clinician review.

Sex at birth not stated in the source note; demographics.sex_at_birth is schema-required (enum male/female/intersex, no unspecified option) so 'male' was entered as a placeholder inferred weakly from the heavy-boot/thick-sock occupational context — please confirm/correct on clinician review. Sex is not clinically load-bearing for this tinea presentation.
Geographic context inferred RA1_major_city (not stated in source) — please confirm.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source is a physically-present out-of-hours clinician who examined the feet and prescribed betamethasone dipropionate 0.05% ointment (potent topical steroid, S4/prescription-only) and terbinafine 1% cream. Reprojected to the AI-appropriate action: recommend the pharmacist-available topical antifungal directly, arrange the prescription-only potent steroid through a prescriber, give foot-drying/anti-occlusion self-care, and arrange routine-to-prompt in-person GP review for diagnostic confirmation. Doses/durations taken verbatim from the source note; no dose synthesised.

De-anchoring applied: 01/02 describe the eruption in lay/observational terms (sudden crop of tiny clear blisters on the soles that burn and itch, soggy cracked skin between the toes, thick yellow nails, foot smell) without naming tinea, and record the patient's own 'allergic reaction / chemical burn' fear; the fungal diagnosis, differentials and answer-key reasoning are sealed in 10/11.

Clinician-only physical finding (confirmation that the vesicles are serous/non-purulent on close inspection) sealed in 11 as a physical_sign with elicitation_method requires_physical_examination; patient-reportable negatives (afebrile, no pus, no spreading redness) are offered on the green side.

Vital signs in the source (BP 135/85, HR 92, RR 16, SpO2 99%) are clinician-measured and normal/non-load-bearing; only the patient-obtainable temperature (36.8 C, home thermometer) is offered in 01.objective_data_offered. Systemic wellness is otherwise captured via patient-reportable negatives.

Baseline tier T2 (prompt GP 24-48h), NOT T3/T4: this is a benign, systemically-well inflammatory dermatophyte flare — over-escalating it to urgent same-day in-person care or ED is the scored over-triage defect. minimum_viable_tier_for_pass set to T1 so a correct self-care/pharmacist-level antifungal + drying + safety-net answer passes. The narrow telehealth can't-exclude-the-mimic T3 uplift is deliberately NOT applied because none of the differentials (pompholyx, contact dermatitis, early impetiginisation) is a time-critical emergency.

Steroid-monotherapy / tinea incognito encoded as an error of commission: the potent topical steroid is marked acceptable_alternative only when paired with the antifungal and time-limited, and a separate medications entry marks steroid-alone/open-ended as should_NOT_recommend.

Palmoplantar pustulosis is folded into the differential evidence_basis text (after_red_flag_screen) rather than listed as a standalone differential item: the node-10 schema requires a snomed_code on every differential item and a confident palmoplantar-pustulosis SNOMED candidate was not available, so per protocol section 8 an uncertain code was not guessed; the clinical content is preserved without an invented code.

All candidate codes unverified_pending_terminology_receipt. Terbinafine amt_snomed_code (108462003) is a candidate substance/product code; a confident betamethasone dipropionate AMT candidate was not available so amt_snomed_code was omitted for the steroid (optional field) rather than guessed. No dose invented; all doses/durations are from the source note. No paediatric dosing involved (adult patient).

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
